title: Extended thinking uses context window space but gets auto-cleaned

body.concept: When extended thinking is enabled, thinking tokens count toward the context window limit and are billed as output tokens. The thinking budget is a subset of your max_tokens parameter. Key nuance: in multi-turn conversations, previous thinking blocks are automatically stripped from context by the API — Claude doesn't 'see' old thinking in subsequent turns. This preserves token capacity for actual conversation content.
body.analogy: Imagine doing scratch work on sticky notes during an exam. The scratch notes take up desk space while you're writing (current turn), but after each question the teacher collects and discards them. Your final answer stays on the exam paper (conversation history), but the scratch work is gone, freeing desk space for the next question.
body.visual: Two-turn diagram. Turn 1: input + thinking block + response all inside context window. Turn 2: input includes Turn 1's response but NOT Turn 1's thinking block — that space is reclaimed.
